version_pin_rationale: xkb-data 2.33-1 (Architecture: all) is the jammy release. THIS WAS THE HIDDEN DE0-G DEP CALLED OUT BY THE DE0-G REPORT — libxkbcommon hard-codes the lookup prefix /usr/share/X11/xkb/ at compile time; without xkb-data Hyprland/sway emit 'no keymap found' and freeze on first input event. Catalog ships the canonical evdev/base rules + pc + us layout + the four xkb dataset directories (compat, geometry, keycodes, symbols, types) that libxkbcommon's keymap-compile path actually reads. DE-H1's build script writes a /etc/profile.d/xkb-data.sh exporting XKB_CONFIG_ROOT=/opt/reproos-linux/store/<hash>/usr/share/X11/xkb so libxkbcommon picks up the overlay path at runtime — sourced by repro-start-hyprland.sh and any TTY login. expected_files[] hand-picks 9 critical files; the .deb ships ~5000 layout files total, all extracted under the store dir by dpkg-deb -x but only these 9 are gated by the integration test.
